fields.description:
## Context

Rebar is a reusable ticket, reconciliation, and review-gate system consumed through several harnesses and deployed into projects with very different stacks, repository shapes, platforms, and execution environments. Development of rebar itself happens primarily in a Python repository on macOS through current agent harnesses, which creates a risk that a technically plausible plan silently bakes rebar-project assumptions into reusable behavior.

Representative failures include a core `rebar landing` command that encodes this repository Gerrit policy, Python-only review rules that fail to apply to a Next.js client, macOS-specific dependencies, and operations that assume the process starts inside a local project checkout. The review must catch concrete incompatibilities without requiring every plan to contain generic portability prose.

## Why

Rebar maintainers need an early, high-recall warning when a proposed plan would break a supported client shape. Downstream clients need project-specific policy to remain in project configuration while reusable mechanisms remain usable across harnesses, target projects, platforms, execution venues, and project-location models. Dogfooding the existing project-criterion and project-coaching seams also provides a real example for client authors.

## What

Add one activated project plan-review criterion, `project.portability`, through `.rebar/criteria_routing.json` and `.rebar/prompts/plan-review-project-portability.md`. It runs in Pass 1 at both container and leaf scope. It does not adjudicate validity, impact, severity, or disposition; existing Pass 2 and Pass 3 do that without modification.

The finder may emit a portability finding only when it supplies all four elements:

- a citation to the explicit plan detail or mechanism carrying the assumption;
- a materially different supported client shape;
- the causal mechanism by which that shape fails; and
- a specific, observable breakage scenario that Pass 2 can verify.

The compatibility counterexample matrix is:

- Harness: Python library, CLI, and remote MCP clients, without assumptions specific to Claude Code, Codex, or another development harness.
- Target project: Ruby, Python, Java, Next.js, .NET, and atypical layouts such as Terraform subprojects inside a monorepo.
- Platform and venue: macOS, Windows, Linux, and BSD across CI, servers, and developer workstations.
- Project location and access: in-checkout current working directory, explicitly located workspace, and server execution outside the target checkout without assuming unrestricted local filesystem access.

Silence about portability is not a finding. If the reviewer cannot construct the cited, concrete counterexample, it emits no finding. Project-specific behavior is allowed when it remains project configuration rather than leaking into reusable core behavior.

Add one project-specific Pass-4 move in `.rebar/plan_review_moves.json`, applicable only when `project.portability` survives review:

- id: `project-portability`
- name: `restore rebar portability`
- applies_when: `["project.portability"]`
- template: `Rework {subject} so it remains portable across supported rebar client shapes; keep project-specific behavior in project configuration or an explicit extension boundary.`

The existing Pass-4 applicability filter, move picker, subject validator, and deterministic renderer remain unchanged. No generic core coaching move is added.

## Approach and sequencing

1. Before implementation, use a fresh worktree based on current `origin/main` to run an offline experiment with temporary project overlay, prompt, and move files. Exercise the real registry, routing, ProductionBatchRunner project fan-in, move loader, applicability filter, and deterministic coaching renderer. Record the command and output on this epic. This de-risks the extension mechanism; it does not become production code.
2. Add the rebar-project overlay, contract-bearing criterion prompt, and scoped project move as one coupled vertical slice. The criterion and move are individually incomplete: the criterion supplies the trigger and the move supplies its tailored remediation.
3. Add focused tests with positive cases for Gerrit landing policy, Python-only criteria, macOS-only dependencies, and current-working-directory assumptions, plus negative cases proving that absent portability prose and properly project-scoped configuration do not create findings. Cover both leaf and container routing.
4. Document the committed rebar configuration as the client-facing dogfood example and run focused plus repository-wide gates.

This approach uses the existing project extension seams instead of changing the generic four-pass kernel. It directly demonstrates the intended boundary, limits blast radius, and preserves Pass 2 and Pass 3 behavior.

## Scope

In scope:

- Rebar-project Pass-1 criterion configuration, prompt, and activation.
- Rebar-project Pass-4 move configuration restricted to `project.portability`.
- Offline wiring experiment in a fresh worktree.
- Focused positive, negative, leaf, container, routing, and rendering tests.
- Documentation of the project configuration as an example for clients.

Out of scope:

- Remediating existing portability debt discovered by the criterion.
- Adding `rebar landing` or other rebar-project workflow policy to reusable core.
- A generic built-in coaching move.
- Changes to Pass 2 validity/impact adjudication or Pass 3 deterministic decisions.
- Guaranteeing that every existing rebar feature already supports every matrix cell.

## Success Criteria

- The rebar project owns and activates `project.portability`, and the production Pass-1 routing includes it for both container and leaf plans.
- The project move loads through the production registry, is offered only for findings carrying `project.portability`, and renders the locked project template.
- Positive and negative fixtures demonstrate concrete counterexample discovery without a missing-prose portability tax.
- Existing plan-review behavior remains unchanged when a client has no project overlay or project move file.
- The experiment evidence and implementation documentation show client authors how the two project extension seams compose.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] The project overlay activates `project.portability` for both leaf/container scopes. Verify: `.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q tests/unit/test_project_portability.py -k criterion_activation`
- [ ] The contract-bearing portability prompt resolves through the project prompt library. Verify: `.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q tests/unit/test_project_portability.py -k prompt_resolution`
- [ ] ProductionBatchRunner includes `project.portability` in project Pass-1 fan-in. Verify: `.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q tests/unit/test_project_portability.py -k fan_in`
- [ ] Every positive fixture surfaces the four-part finding contract: plan citation, alternate client shape, causal failure mechanism, observable breakage scenario. Verify: `.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q tests/unit/test_project_portability.py -k positive_counterexample_contract`
- [ ] A plan with no portability discussion produces no `project.portability` finding. Verify: `.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q tests/unit/test_project_portability.py -k omission_is_not_a_finding`
- [ ] A plan that keeps specialization in project configuration produces no `project.portability` finding. Verify: `.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q tests/unit/test_project_portability.py -k configured_specialization_is_portable`
- [ ] The `project-portability` move is applicable exclusively when `project.portability` survives review. Verify: `.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q tests/unit/test_project_portability.py -k coaching_applicability`
- [ ] The `project-portability` move deterministically renders the locked project template. Verify: `.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q tests/unit/test_project_portability.py -k coaching_render`
- [ ] [operator-attested] A fresh `origin/main` worktree experiment proves criterion activation through Pass-1 fan-in plus project-move applicability/rendering. Evidence recorded on this epic must name the worktree path, branch, base SHA, exact commands, success assertions, output, and timestamp.
- [ ] Overlay-absent plan-review behavior remains green. Verify: `.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q tests/unit/test_criteria_overlay.py tests/unit/test_plan_review.py`
- [ ] `docs/plan-review-gate.md` documents the committed rebar project configuration as a client-facing dogfood example. Verify: `.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q tests/unit/test_project_portability.py -k documentation_example`
- [ ] Repository gates pass. Verify: `make lint && make typecheck && make test`
